mnemonics: CAP Theorem: You can't have Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance all at the same time. Remember: Choose Two Per system.
keyPoints: A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appears to its users as a single coherent system.
Key enabling technologies include networking, middleware, and virtualization.
The CAP theorem is a fundamental concept stating a distributed system can only guarantee two out of three: Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance.
commonMistakes: Assuming distributed systems are the same as parallel systems (they are not; distributed systems are loosely coupled over a network).
Believing strong consistency is always required or achievable in a globally distributed system (it often isn't, leading to designs favoring eventual consistency).
